steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: The left side is an independent clause ending at the blank (...is one of the longest suspension bridges in the ______). The right side is a separate independent clause (the Yavuz Sultan Selim Bridge...is even longer). Two independent clauses must be properly connected.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
A comma + coordinating conjunction correctly joins two independent clauses.

The contrast between the sentences (one bridge ranks among the longest; the other is longer) indicates but as the right conjunction, so world, but is correct.

A) adds an unwanted comma after but, B) omits the conjunction (creating a comma splice), and D) drops the required comma before but—all violate the joining rule.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
